rationale: Classification by escalation shape (Arbiter does not read content for decisioning): severity_estimate='cross-section-architectural'; type='schema_coverage_gap' is not in the canonical type taxonomy (contract_data_shape | contract_semantics | approach_conflict | assumption_violated | unresolvable) — it is an architecture-level property-check question about file_schemas.md's strict-walker semantics; proposed_resolution.type='v1_10_schema_clarification' explicitly targets the architecture document, not any in-run artifact. section_id is null (not section-scoped). The remediation options enumerated (codify transitive coverage, require new DCA entries, normalize covers-pointer suffix conventions) all modify architecture-level schemas or TD authoring rules — neither Coordinator nor TD has authority to amend file_schemas.md mid-run, and Discovery has not attempted an amendment whose rejection would drop this to Sev 3. By the Arbiter charter's classification ladder this is Sev 4 (irreconcilable at the run level; requires Orchestrator surfacing for architecture-level resolution). Per the charter's 'if unsure, classify higher' rule and the in-run pragmatic note that the build itself is correct under the spirit of Principle C (the underlying decisions ARE covered transitively via chosen_branch), the architecture's strict-walker behavior is what needs refinement — that is an out-of-band v1.10 amendment, not an in-run fix.
expected_resolution_path: Orchestrator receives this routing, treats the strict-walker finding as a recommended_action='v1_10_amendment_candidate', and records it in runs/streamdock-apple-music-touchbar/run-report.md's Uncertainty Manifest / Architecture Learnings section so it carries forward to v1.10 design. The in-run build is not blocked: spirit-of-coverage holds (IP1-IP4 default_branch is covered transitively via inflection_resolutions[].chosen_branch, per esc-002.evidence). The minor anomaly (DCA.FC.3 covers-pointer suffix omission) is noted for the same amendment cycle. Orchestrator does not pause delivery; this routing is informational for the post-run reflection and the architecture's evolution.
